title: hieracium dilutius
parts: Hieracium dilutius has many medicinal parts, such as leaves, flowers, and roots, which contain various bioactive compounds. The leaves of Hieracium dilutius contain flavonoids, including quercetin and kaempferol, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The flowers of the plant contain sesquiterpene lactones, including 11,13-dihydrohelenin and helenin, which have been shown to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The roots of Hieracium dilutius contain phenolic acids, including ferulic and sinapic acid, which have been reported to have antioxidant and anti-cancer properties.
side_effects: Hieracium dilutius can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing allergic reactions, digestive issues, and respiratory problems in some individuals. Prolonged use or excessive consumption of the plant can lead to nausea, diarrhea, and stomach cramps. In severe cases, misuse of Hieracium dilutius may cause cardiovascular issues, including elevated heart rate and blood pressure. It is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using the plant for medicinal purposes to avoid these potential side effects.
